Content: Alright, my schedule for the day is cleared. Now I just...
Long time no see, Platya.
Here to experience Academy life too?
{PlayerName}, it's been a while! After the others at the Black Shores learned you were going to Startorch Academy, everyone's been worried about how you're settling in.
The things you've been dealing with... we've learned bits and pieces from the Collective's briefings.
You haven't formally requested assistance. That tells us you want to handle this yourself. And we trust you.
After what happened to Aemeath... and with the threat of Aleph-1, I know now isn't the time to be putting everything on the line.
But I've made up my mind. I will bring her back. That's my burden, and I can't drag the Collective or the Black Shores into it.
If you're sure that's what you want to do, then please, let us help. Okay?
Yeah. Of course.
But you didn't come all this way just to say that, did you?
Ahem, no. This is a little embarrassing, heh... I actually have a personal favor to ask.
I'm here for the Black Shores–Spacetrek Collective exchange program. But I'm also here for personal reasons.
You've been living at Startorch for a while now, so I thought you could help. But if it's inconvenient, just say so! Really, it's okay!
It's no trouble.
Tell me about it first.
What, do I look like the type to mope around and make everyone fuss over me? Go on. I want to help if I can.
Mm!
It's about a miko from my hometown. I don't know if you've heard, but Ashinohara's defense against the Threnodian was built around mikos.
Among them, there was one who was special. She bore the title "Flaming Sakura," but to us, she was just the "Lady Miko."
When I was a child, I caught a glimpse of her once. Just her back.
She was gazing at a giant tree covered in wish slips. The wind blew her long sleeves like a kite with a broken string.
Then she turned. Did she see me? I don't know. Maybe it was the distance. Maybe it's just my memory. But... I can't remember her face anymore.
What happened after that?
Later... Honami fell. While I tried to escape, I encountered a swarm of Tacet Discords. And all I could think was—I don't want to die.
That's when I saw her again. That same silhouette. She only said one thing to me: "Run. Live."
Even at the very end, the Lady Miko was doing everything she could to protect us.
Since then, there hasn't been any news of her. Not until recently, when I heard from another Bloom Bearer about a legend of a "Ghost Miko" in Lahai-Roi.
The descriptions match the "Lady Miko." So I figured, since I'm here for the exchange, I'd try to find out more.
Actually... during a recent incident at the Academy, I met a miko. She helped me out, then disappeared.
(And Hiyuki mentioned in her last message that she's been searching for traces of Aemeath. But she did ask me to keep that quiet for now.)
I don't know if she's the Lady Miko you're talking about.
Ultimate Psych Rep invited {PlayerName} to the group chat
OKAY SO did y'all hear?? The Ghost Miko showed up at the Film Club room again. Sometime after 11:00 PM.
And she watched THAT film again—"Leon the Unprofessional"!
Is this some kind of rule-based creepypasta?
Tell me more.
Is this some kind of rule-based creepypasta?
A Ghost Miko... and films? What's the deal?
K newbie. Guess you don't know. Aside from Startorch's Seven Great Mysteries, there's the legend of the Film-Loving Ghost Miko.
They say the Film Club room has a stash of classic DVDs. Late at night, when no one's supposed to be there, you can hear "clack clack" sounds from inside...
And then, a figure in miko robes just sits in the middle of the room and watches a film. When it's over, the door cam shows no one going in or out.
So the only explanation...
LEVITATING GHOST. Obviously.
Why that film specifically?
Why is it always "Leon the Unprofessional"?
Well... maybe the ghost was killed by a hitman! Like, her trauma's so strong she's cursed to rewatch it forever?
Or she was just really into films when she was alive, and now she's dead and bored. Gotta fill the afterlife somehow.
But either way, newbie, don't think you can go poking around there hoping for a chat.
We can't guarantee your safety. Wait, what's that line again...??
When you gaze into the abyss, the abyss gazes back. Never get too close to legends.
You mean...
The people closest to the legend are the ones spreading it, right?
True. According to those students, the Lady Miko might appear in the club room after 11:00 tonight. So I was thinking...
You want to go see for yourself.
Yes. Even if it's just a legend, I want to try.
{PlayerName}, let's meet in the club room after 11:00 tonight.
You catch wind of a "Film-Loving Ghost Miko" urban legend in the Academy's group chat. You decide to start investigating there.
{PlayerName}, did you see anyone else on your way here?
No one.
...So she didn't come after all.
I actually got here early, just in case the timing in the legend wasn't exact. But I guess...
Forget it. Since we're here, we may as well watch this film.
{PlayerName}, perfect timing! I met a Roya at the exchange meeting just now.
He mentioned that a Soliskin guarded by their tribe seems to have made a mysterious friend. It's been coming home late, acting strangely.
More importantly, once when a Roya Shepherd went to look for the Soliskin, he heard the sound of a bell.
He followed the sound and found the Soliskin fast asleep in the grass. And no sound of the bell.
{PlayerName}, I think...
It's your Lady Miko, isn't it?
Yes. When I was little, I went with my parents to make a wish to the Lady Miko. I heard the sound of a bell, too.
{PlayerName}, would you go to Bjartr Woods in my place? For some reason, the Soliskin seem a little scared of me...
Leave it to me.
I'll give it a shot.
Chu, lulu, lulu? Lululu! (*Happy sounds*)
Are you inviting me to walk with you?
Lulu, chu!
Chu, lulu, chuchu! Lulu! (*Excited sounds*)
This cup of tea... is for me?
You watch as the Soliskin holds up a cup of tea. Though made from a tea bag, the aroma is remarkably fresh and fragrant.
Lulu!
(Take the teacup)
(Taste it)
Chu, lulu.
Do you and your mysterious friend drink tea here? This cup... Your friend left it for me, didn't she?
Chu? Lulu, chu!
(She knows Platya and I are tracking her. She knew I was coming today.)
Tastes very nice. Thank you and your friend for the hospitality. Well then... bye-bye, little one.
Chu, lulu, chuchu! Lulu! (*Excited sounds*)
{PlayerName}, any new leads?
(Tell her about the Soliskin.)
I see. My father once mentioned that serving tea to guests is part of a miko's social etiquette.
And... do you think she already knows we're looking for her? What if she's been close by this whole time?
We just haven't noticed...
It's possible.
She could be tracking us some other way.
But if that's true, then finding her just got way harder. We haven't sensed a thing.
True...
Thank you for today, {PlayerName}. Let's see if anything new turns up tomorrow.
Still nothing... Ugh. No, no, I need to stay positive.
What's wrong?
Well, after we parted yesterday, I tried to pick up the Lady Miko's trail on my own, but didn't find much.
And... do you think the Academy's noticed I've been looking into her? Suddenly my exchange schedule is packed with new activities.
Maybe now just isn't the right time.
Maybe the Lady Miko has her reasons.
You... really think so?
Like you said, she spent her life protecting everyone in Honami. She wouldn't deliberately avoid someone she cared about without a reason.
Maybe for now... the timing just isn't right.
...You're right. I'll focus on my exchange work for the Black Shores and the Collective.
But I still have one small wish. If I can't meet the Lady Miko in person, I want to prepare a gift for her. Something to say thank you.
I know just the person to help you deliver it.
Hmm... but what kind of gift? I only know about the Lady Miko from stories. I don't know what she actually likes.
You could try asking other people's opinions?
Other people's?
Yes, like someone from your hometown. Or people here in Lahai-Roi connected to the Ghost Miko legend.
That's true. More input means more ideas.
I know who to ask.
I have someone in mind.
(Maybe I can ask Chisa. She stayed in Honami for a long time.)
(She should know something about this miko.)
Hello, {PlayerName}. What is it you wanted to discuss?
Any idea what kinds of things the miko of Honami likes?
The Lady Miko...? I didn't have much contact with her. As a child, I just followed my parents to make wishes.
I was... scared of her back then. I always hid behind my mother's dress.
But when we were trapped in Honami, I remember hearing some of the Left Behind talk about her.
Back then, when someone came to make a wish, they'd bring her pastries. Different ones, every time.
The Lady Miko rarely appeared on the streets, you see. The city changed too fast. Made her feel like a stranger.
So she experienced Honami through taste. The flavors changed with the times, and through them, she understood what Honami loved.
Pastries. Noted...
Why the sudden interest?
It has to do with someone's wish.
It has to do with someone's secret.
...Oh, you're helping someone fulfill a wish again? Someone the Lady Miko protected? Is there anything I can do to help?
Not for now.
Alright. I don't know what you're planning, but I do have one small suggestion.
When I was acting as the cafe manager, I learned something. Knowing what the customer likes is important.
But conveying what you want them to feel? That's more important.
What you want them to feel...
If you need anything, call me. Anytime.
You're back, {PlayerName}.
(Tell her about Chisa's information.)
Pastries... that's a good idea. But I've never baked before. The taste or look of it might not turn out right...
Chisa also had some advice. She said what you want her to feel matters more than just what she likes.
What I want to tell the Lady Miko...
Try putting everything you're thankful for into the gift.
I'll try. The exchange program's almost over, so...
That's it for now, {PlayerName}. I need to hurry.
{PlayerName}, there you are. So I've asked around, and it turns out the initial sightings of the Ghost Miko didn't happen on campus.
They were in the Roya Frostlands. Quite a few researchers mentioned being saved by a figure dressed in plain white robes.
I'm convinced that the figure in the frostlands, this Ghost Miko, is the Lady Miko I'm looking for.
Some of them tried talking to her, you know? Shouting into the blizzard. But she never answered.
Only one researcher accidentally found an ID card where she'd fought a few Tacet Discords. Translated from the Lahai-Roi language, it read—"Special Response Force."
No name. No photo. Aside from that, the card was blank.
I've never heard of that force before.
But since it translates from the Lahai-Roi language, that proves the Ghost Miko has ties to the Spacetrek Collective.
I remember there's an AI in the Academy that can access Spacetrek Collective data. Maybe we can ask her?
You mean I.R.I.S.?
Exactly! So you've already met her. I'll leave talking with I.R.I.S. to you, then.
I'll keep poking around campus. See what else I can dig up.
...So tired. So, so tired...
Those are some serious dark circles.
Yeah, yeah... {PlayerName}, here. Please give this to the Lady Miko for me.
Did you stay up all night?
...Hehe. Yeah, I've never baked before, and my hand-eye coordination isn't great, so... it took quite a few tries to get it right
But don't worry! I'm really proud of the final look and taste.
And... from everything I've learned about her these past few days, I don't think she'd like anything too strong or overly sweet.
So I asked an Academy mech to dig through the archives. Found this ingredient that tones down the sweetness. Makes it taste lighter, more balanced.
Leave its delivery to me then.
Thank you. I guess... this is goodbye for now. After today's schedule wraps up, I'm heading back to the Black Shores.
I hope everything goes well for you at Startorch. Really.
This memory... is redundant. I'll erase that...
Oh? {PlayerName}. Here for some life advice? Come to chat with the President?
It's about that miko.
She reached out to you, huh? I told her she shouldn't be going underground anymore. Seems she's acting on her own anyway.
You don't plan to misbehave too, do you?
(Hand Lucilla the gift from Platya.)
That's not why I'm here. The Exostrider is critical not just to the Collective, but to all of Lahai-Roi. I wouldn't act without authorization. Not for "my own" sake.
This is a gift from someone the miko once protected. Handmade. Think you could pass it to her?
And tell her I do want to save Aemeath. More than anything. But I don't want anyone else getting hurt for it either.
Hah... You two might actually get along. Which is saying something. Hiyuki doesn't exactly do small talk.
Both of you have a "savior complex." As an educator, I'm here to tell you that isn't a good thing.
At any rate, I'll make sure your friend's gift reaches her. And your request to activate the Exostrider... Fine. I'll see what I can do.
I've already set a timeline with Hiyuki. This next trip is the last one the Collective is authorizing.
That also means, until your own request is approved, you wait. Okay?
I understand.
Oh, and one correction. It's not just for "your own" sake. It's for "my student." And more so, for "everyone Aleph-1 ever hurt."
The Voidmatter in the Dimmr Plains beneath Lahai-Roi is growing increasingly unstable. We don't have much time... Go on. When I have news, I'll contact you.

Weather: Sunny
Log: All systems normal
I'm not good at writing these. But last month, when I didn't submit one... she seemed upset.
This should be enough to meet the word count. Everything's fine. Don't worry, I'll make sure everything stays fine.
{PlayerName}? Is something the matter?
I have something I want to discuss with you.
I have something I want to discuss with you. Can't explain it quickly over text. I'll come find you.
I see. I'll wait for you near the Academy Plaza then.
Hi {PlayerName}, this is Platya. How have things been?
I've arrived at Startorch Academy. If you have time, let's meet up. I'll be waiting for you at the entrance of Rabelle College.
{PlayerName}, I've got the intel sorted. Is now a good time to meet?
Same place, near Rabelle College.
Spacetrek Archive Retrieval AI, I.R.I.S. {PlayerName}, long time no see. What brings you to see me today?
Search "Special Response Force."
Searching... 13 results match your query.
Summarize key information from all results.
Affirmative! Executing operation. The "Special Response Force" was an emergency response team established by the Spacetrek Collective in the past.
Unlike Startorch Academy, they didn't care about research or philosophy. They only had one job.
Eliminate anything that threatened the Collective. Members operated in five-person squads, with year-round deployment in the most dangerous zones imaginable.
Over time, the casualty rate increased annually, reaching as high as 85%.
Then there was an incident. During one operation, all members of the Special Response Force were confirmed KIA.
Now, they technically still exist. But the system shows no new members have joined since.
That's all the key information. Anything else I can help you with?
Is there information on the members?
I'm sorry. The system contains no records regarding specific members.
Could there have been survivors from that operation?
Answering hypotheticals isn't really my hobby, {PlayerName}. But if that's your guess... Gotta say, it is possible.
If someone did survive, they never reported back. Or maybe the Collective never updated the data. Either way, the system still shows 100% casualties in that last operation.
Understood. Thank you.
Sure thing. You know where to find me if you have any more questions.
I think the students guessed wrong. The reason the Lady Miko keeps watching this movie... is because she sees herself in the hitman.
Back in Honami, and now here in Lahai-Roi, her life revolves around the duty to protect. And always at a distance.
After all these days of investigating, I may not have seen her with my own eyes, but the blurry figure from my memories is slowly becoming clearer.
It's late. Let's call it here for today.
You watch "Leon the Unprofessional" with Platya...
So? How'd it go? Did I.R.I.S. point you in the right direction?
(Relay the information  I.R.I.S. provided.)
So just the basics about the task force's history. No actual member records at all...
But... I think I.R.I.S. made a good point at the end. What if the Lady Miko survived, but the Collective's system never recorded her?
Or what if she made some kind of pact with the Collective? For instance... she didn't want anyone else to get hurt, so she decided the Special Response Force needed only her.
After all, the Lady Miko I knew... was exactly that kind of person.
Thanks for your help today, {PlayerName}. Let's call it a day. Tomorrow we can brainstorm other angles to try.
{PlayerName}, I found something! Some researchers have encountered a mysterious miko during their expeditions. Which means... maybe the expedition camps have clues!
I just sent you the coordinates for one of the camps. The others... Well, I can't keep bothering you forever. I'll check those out myself.
See you later then.
Try not to get lost.
Mhm! See you later. Hope we both find something good.
Don't worry about me. Navigating complex terrain is a mandatory course in the Black Shores' Bloom Bearer training.
If you're ready, let's move out.

Today, I walked through the valley of the shadow of death. I was surrounded by a swarm of Tacet Discords.
Right when I'd given up hope, I heard a noise. It wasn't the wind, but a crisp, rhythmic "ding... ding..." like a sharp blade tapping against ice.
I forced my eyes open. They were practically sealed shut with the frost.

A figure appeared before me. A girl, I think. White robes. She held a long, straight blade made of ice, transparent and clear. As she moved, the tip would occasionally graze the ground, making that cold "ding-ding" sound.
 She didn't say a word. Just walked toward the swarm. The snow swallowed her fast. I heard the "ding... ding..." of her blade against the ground a few more times before everything fell silent. When I looked again, both she and the Discords were gone.
 
I didn't think. I just ran. When I got back to camp, my colleagues  looked at me like they'd seen a ghost. Turns out I'd been missing for four hours. I told them everything. They said it was a hallucination.
So... was it?
Does a woman with a blade of ice truly walk the frostlands of Lahai-Roi?
Maybe I should go back there tomorrow and take another look.
{PlayerName}, I'm done with my side of the investigation.
Let's meet up at our usual spot?
Hey, you made it. So here's what I found. Honestly, it's not much.
The places I checked were full of other people's information. It was too messy to pick out anything useful.
But I did find one journal. The daily habits recorded in it match someone from Ashinohara. It's highly likely our Lady Miko wrote it herself.
In it, she reminisced about one busy day in the past—what she ate for breakfast, lunch, dinner, as well as ceremonial dress etiquette...
She laid out her entire schedule for that day in detail. Didn't specify the date, though.
But I think... that day must have been a commemorative festival in Honami. Only a miko like herself would know those rituals that well.
Being a miko sounds exhausting.
Mikos must have a high status in Honami.
Of course. To us in Honami, the Lady Miko was like... the Blessed Maiden in Rinascita.
When I was little, I looked up to her so much. But as I got older, I realized being a miko isn't all sunshine and roses... Anyway, not the point.
(Tell her the information you found.)
I see... so one of the journals must've belonged to a researcher. He wrote about being saved by her.
As for the other one... We can't know who wrote it, but what they wrote gave me a hint.
It mentioned being required by "someone" to write the report. Didn't seem like they had restrictions on where they went, just that they needed to check in regularly.
I wonder... could the Lady Miko have the same kind of arrangement with the Spacetrek Collective?
They have to know she exists. What if she made some kind of deal with them?
Which means, she's probably still in regular contact with someone here.
(Sounds exactly like the miko I met.)
What is it, {PlayerName}?
Nothing. I think your guess is right. I met her before while resolving one of the Academy's incidents.
I was curious about who she was back then, but the person I was with at the time said...
(Imitate Lucilla's tone.)
"About that miko lady you're curious about..."
"My lips are sealed on that, I'm afraid. A President has her principles, not to mention the Academy's safety protocol. But one day, you'll see her again."
Don't give me those puppy dog eyes. Even if you went and asked the President yourself, she wouldn't tell you.
...If you say so. But with all these coincidences lining up, there's no doubt in my mind that the Lady Miko is at the Academy.
I'll figure out another way to track her down. Appreciate your help today, really.
Speaking of which, we've only been chasing footprints she left in the past. I know very little about her present.
Any ideas, {PlayerName}? How do we track down her current location?
Give me a second.
There might be clues in the student group chat.
If the Ghost Miko really is her, I want to find and thank her in person.
Really?! So she's not a ghost after all! There really is a miko in this Academy! {PlayerName}, you know this place well. Can you help me investigate?
What do you need from me?
I've actually gathered some clues already. Done a little prep work.  But give me a little time to organize everything.
Once I've gone through it all, we can discuss our next move.
Sorry to bother you at this hour, {PlayerName}.
Wait, the President is using a cute emoji?
Well, seems our dear student has quite an "interesting" bias against the faculty.
Maybe I should add a new course. Startorch Guidelines: From Strangers to Confidants.
As for its final exam, what about "How to spend a wonderful day with your teacher"?
Please tell me that's not a required course.
I've got enough classes to survive...
Jokes aside. Let's get down to business. I want to discuss the Academy's Dark Side with you.
I'll be waiting for you on Rabelle College's top floor. Come see me whenever you can.
You receive a WavesLine message from Lucilla.
The President using cute emojis? No way. Who are you, really?
Please tell me that's not a required course.
No way. I've got enough classes to survive...
About what Professor Karl just said...
Don't worry. I'm fine. In my position, hearing skeptical voices is part of the job.
But tell me, {PlayerName}, what do you think a President should be like, and where should Startorch Academy be heading?
No rush. Go see it for yourself. Let's keep moving. Professor Sevi should be just ahead.
You're late. Did you bring all the materials I asked for?
Hand me the recalibrator... Hm? What—
Ah, my mistake. I thought you were my assistant. You are... Have we met before?
...Ah, the Academy newspaper! You must be the student behind the success of the New Solar Ceremony, right?
Pleasure to meet you. I'm {PlayerName}.
Yes, I've heard everything.
Looks like Professor Sevi didn't want to open up about what he saw in the Dark Side.
Even so, that medal's a valuable lead. It's deeply connected to him.
Please place the medal in front of the makeup mirror. I can't touch it myself, so I'll read and tune its frequency remotely.
Excellent. Now, leave the rest to the adult.
Do as Lucilla says.
I need more time.
Using the medal you just received, Lucilla reads Professor Sevi's past memories.
...I can't even feel time passing here. Half an hour's gone already?
This one... should go here. Wait, the rabbit—
You again! Are you the one who changed the riddle and warped this place? Get back here!
This memory records the moment he met the rabbit. Before that, he mentioned time. Could that be another key factor?
Did the other victims have similar behaviors?
Let's not jump to conclusions yet. Keep going.
Gone again? No matter how good you are at hide-and-seek, your traces stay in my memory.
I'll just need to use my Forte again.
Surprised? Staring at yourself.
Y-you are... No, I know what this is. The Dark Side's a mirror. You're just my shadow.
Hah. Even if I'm a shadow, so what? I'm still part of you. Afraid your shadow might know a few of your secrets?
Secrets you've been running from...
You're trapped, desperate for progress, like that Huanglong researcher, who eventually threw everything away for the truth.
Pascar Spectrum... That name rings a bell?
...I'm nothing like Pascar.
Oh, you're right about that. You don't even have his courage to give it all up. That's why you're stuck here, like a lab rat running in circles.
But what if I told you... the answer you've been seeking is right here? Something about your current project. Go on, take a look.
The data model... was wrong from the start? Is this where the error lies? If I fix it, my second hypothesis could actually work.
Then, based on the corrected model, the result should be... Wait, there's a formula missing? The first half matches my derivation perfectly...
Where's the rest? Where's the other half of the formula?
No use hiding. I know you're there.

In the Dark Side, Professor Sevi encountered the rabbit and realized it was responsible for harming students, so he chased after it. 
When he activated his memory-type Forte, the rabbit transformed into him, trying to break his mind.
So that's why Professor Sevi was so determined to keep everyone out of the Dark Side...
He was protecting them in his own way. He feared that eager students or passionate teachers might trade their lives for an illusion of truth.
Greatness isn't just about achievement. Sometimes, it's about facing reality and still choosing to live with dignity.
At Startorch Academy, we pursue truth, yes, but we value the journey more than the destination. Weighing our worth on a scale? That's true ignorance.
Now then, if we cross-check the clues from Sevi with the Academy's earlier findings...
You're half right. The key isn't the Forte itself. It's what it acts upon.
In both cases, they influenced the space itself. That's the real link.
The rabbit's appearance is linked to time?
The rabbit's appearance is linked to the victim?
The rabbit's appearance is linked to using Forte?
Spending more time in the Dark Side does raise the chance of seeing the rabbit, but that's not the root cause.
You're saying it's drawn to one's vulnerability.
But everyone has weaknesses. Some hide them deeper, some don't. That alone isn't special enough.
Remember? Professor Sevi used his Forte to restore the scene, and other student victims used theirs to take something from the Dark Side...
Lucilla understands why Sevi stayed silent. But her investigation also uncovered a pattern: the rabbit only appears when someone uses a memory-type Forte in the Dark Side.
Since the Fractsidus went to such lengths to invite me, well, as the President, how could I turn down the feast?
So you knew it was a trap all along...
Of course. And I knew if I'd told you sooner, you'd either stop me or try to follow me.
But there's something only you can do from the outside.
The Dark Side is unstable by nature. Remember what I said? The Academy used riddles to regulate its gateways.
Most have been replaced by Fractsidus riddles, but a few originals are still hidden in the Academy's archive.
And the archive is... inclusive. Be careful what you dig into.
{PlayerName}, I need you to set a stable Dark Side exit with a riddle unknown to the Fractsidus.
But I can't let you go alone...
Remember what you told Sigrika at the Celebration? That means no matter what, we stick together.
You invited me here, and you can't just ditch me now.
Even if I said no, you'd come anyway, wouldn't you? Fine. The Dark Side is a maze. I'll leave a few clues behind to guide you.
But if anything goes wrong, promise me you'll get out immediately.

The perspective returns to you. Before losing contact, Lucilla asked you to visit the Academy's archive for the backup riddles prepared for the celebration to control access to the Dark Side. Lucilla wants you to use a riddle unknown to the Fractsidus to generate a stable exit. 
Worried for Lucilla's safety, you decide to enter the Dark Side and join her after setting up the exit.
"When the Bird Glides Across the Sky"... that song I listened to during my insomnia last night. The Rinascitans do have an exquisite taste in art.
But this melody just keeps my mind going. Not suitable for sleeping. Erase.
Next... today's breakfast. Hmm, Royan-style.
All trivial. Erase it too. It was delicious, though.
What? Do I look like some murmuring android to you?
Sorry, President. Were you...
Tuning memories. Curious about my Forte?
About your Forte...
Mind giving me a quick explanation?
Everyone leaves behind frequencies on the objects they carry. My Forte allows me to read and analyze that information.
Those frequencies, in a sense, are their memories.
I can read, analyze, and store them in my mind, erasing or adding memories at will. That's what I call "memory tuning."
I can extract frequency information from an object, use it as an anchor to draw out related memories, and store them in my mind.
But the brain has limits, so I have to decide which memories to keep... and which to discard. Just like you saw earlier.
Like intermittent amnesia?
Doesn't that affect your daily life?
It's different from amnesia. With amnesia, memories might still return.
But when I "erase" something through memory tuning, I erase the frequency entirely. There's no recovering it.
Don't worry. I've set clear rules for what I erase. It's part of my daily life now.
Alright, back to the point. I told you all that because it ties into what I mentioned in the message earlier.
You mean the Dark Side?
Are you planning to use your Forte on me?
Correct. I need your help to confirm something. May I borrow an item you always carry?
(You hand her your student card.)
Oh? You still have it. Perfect. Objects with personal meaning carry stronger frequencies.
{PlayerName}, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Let's begin.
I have a general picture now. At the end of the Celebration, you and Sigrika entered the Dark Side to solve the final riddle, and encountered another "Sigrika."
And you realized she was a fake when she said, "Should finding an answer really be so important?"
You got it all right... Amazing how much you learned just from that single card.
To me, your memories play like a film. I can pause and study them frame by frame.
So, what happened after the fake Sigrika revealed herself?
Then... there was a rabbit. I remember chasing it, the same one I saw right after entering the Dark Side.
I see. Thank you, {PlayerName}. I think I've found what I needed.
Alright, looks like my guest has arrived. Gotta handle the rest later.
Remember when I said Mornye compared the frequencies and found that the rabbit was likely the root cause of the Dark Side anomaly?
The rabbit leads those who enter the Dark Side to face what they fear and try to avoid. Just like Sigrika meeting her alter ego.
Since then, the anomaly has persisted. Some students and professors have faced the same situation.

You find a distracted Lucilla on the top floor of Rabelle College. She explains that she was using her Forte to tune her memories. She can read memories off objects, but there's only so much the human brain can remember, so she has to periodically erase memories to make room for new ones. 
To help with the Dark Side investigation, she asks if she can borrow something you're carrying to retrace what you experienced in there.
The alternative riddles for the New Solar Celebration... Guess this is what President Lucilla wanted.
Why is it tucked into a storyboard, though? Her hobby?
Hope this helps.
Enter the Dark Side.
Maybe later.
That's odd... Why are Tacet Discords here? And they're all moving towards her.
You're here. This way...
If I follow this path, it should lead me to the President.
The Tacet Discords are gathering around here...
This is...
Heh, even got my taste right, huh? It's always so easy to read one's mind.
"The Tragic Life of Lucilla," directed by Lucilla, starring Lucilla.
Using my name and slapping on a title that lame?
I heard the Fractsidus had a playwright. And this is what your "art" looks like?
"This is the story of a girl who discovered she could read memories when she started to grow up."
"Before she could fully develop her self-knowledge, she absorbed too many memories that weren't her own... until she lost who she was."
Stealing Sevi's voice, twisting people's lives into your cheap drama...
Since when does that count as cinema? What's next, my grand arrival in Lahai-Roi?
Some time earlier...
Welcome. Enjoy your own movie.

Lucilla follows the rabbit into a movie theater. A title flashes on screen: "The Tragic Life of Lucilla," directed by Lucilla, starring Lucilla. 
The Fractsidus have taken her past, twisted parts out of context, and stitched them all into a movie. It shows her as a child subconsciously absorbing too many memories, until she couldn't tell what was hers anymore and began to lose herself. Then comes the ending they've predicted for her: Lucilla, at the Academy's helm, leading everyone straight into the abyss. 
Lucilla scoffs at the movie's utter lack of taste. Then a familiar voice echoes through the theater. The Fractsidus have decided to remake the film, redesigning Lucilla's life and creating an entirely different version of events.
Now, I'll announce the results of the Startorch Academy presidential election.
The vote was jointly conducted by each department's committee and the New Federation Special Investigation Panel.
Professor Lucilla, congratulations. You've been elected as the new President of Startorch Academy.
Well then, President Lucilla, care to say a few words?
Alright then, page sixty, bottom right corner. This photo was taken before the Lament.
Back then, there was no Etheric Sea, and aerospace technology wasn't blocked yet. One of humanity's probes took this image in space...
Our home world, see? Just a pale blue dot.
So what's the point?
Excuse me?
I mean, what's the point of taking that photo? Looking at it only makes us feel small.
That tiny dot only makes all of us on it feel more insignificant, pathetic even. That so-called golden age of exploration and the great astronomy before the Lament... All they bring is loneliness.
We're all lonely in that endless darkness, just like Startorch Academy now. Tell me, Professor, does our so-called "exploration" here even matter?
I... I don't know...
Professor Lucilla, last time we stopped at page fifty-nine!
In the remake, a student confronts Lucilla in class, questioning the point of exploring the unknown if answers can never truly be known.
Given the Grand Architect's teaser for you and Dr. Luuk, that rabbit should be one of the Fractsidus's little tricks in this game of theirs.
They harmed my students and colleagues with such despicable means... As the President, I'll make sure they get a taste of their own medicine.
What are you planning to do?
That rabbit is their pawn, which means, I can turn it against them.
We'll lure it out, capture it, and remove the anomaly at its source. Then, using my Forte, I can extract information about the Fractsidus from it.
But mobilizing the Academy's resources might alert our enemies. I need some more personal aid—someone I trust, who knows the Dark Side well... and that's you.
So... does that count as my holiday assignment?
Even if you didn't ask, I'd help anyway.
Hehe, let's call it a field project. A joint study between a professor and her dear student.
Wonderful. Hearing that puts me at ease.

Based on Lucilla's current findings, the rabbit in the Dark Side is the source of the anomaly. 
She's come up with a few ways to counter it, and she wants you in her corner.
Absurd! You want to withdraw now, at the most critical stage of the experiment? Who could possibly take your place?
I-I'm just... scared. Scared to touch those instruments again. Scared of seeing results that don't match what we hoped for.
Afraid of the risks? Then switch to theoretical work! Are you really going to let everything you've built amount to nothing?
In the remake, Lucilla quits her research group, fearful of results that don't match expectations. Accepting her own mediocrity, she chooses to stop moving forward.
Professor Lucilla just ran out of the room, clutching her head... like she's lost it.
Are you really quitting the project, Lucilla? You've made up your mind?
...Yes.
This will be my final lecture. I've already filed to close the course next term.
Thank you all for your support. I've gone as far as I can.
I'm sure you'll all go farther than I ever could...
A different choice, a brand-new life. Tell me, our dear audience, isn't this fascinating?
If everything's destined to fade away, why not just wait it out? Enjoy the last bit of pleasure and revel in the beautiful memories before the end.
After all, fleeting joy feels far more real than some unreachable truth.
You only need to come a little closer, and take my hand.
Our lives can trade places. No more pain, only warmth and bliss. The curtain's about to fall, and the choice is yours.
Done with your performance?
All that noise for one purpose, the same trick you used on Sevi. Stirring fear, pressing old wounds.
Let me guess what you're after. Targeting Sigrika was just a diversion. You wanted the Academy to intervene.
Next, the memory-type Resonators affected in the Dark Side, and you knew I'd see it. That was your invitation.
So this little play? It was meant for me and all the memory-type Resonators. You're afraid of something in us, aren't you? Something that threatens your grand scheme.
You put on quite a hilarious show, I'll give you that. But you came to the wrong audience. You can ramble about pasts and futures all you like, but I only focus on the present.
And that's exactly why your "message" in that film disgusts me.
I don't deny the doubt, the struggle, or the pain we face in our search. Every soul in Startorch Academy has endured those.
But the weight of civilization demands shoulders to bear it. And we, right from the beginning, are a flock walking willingly toward our own end.
If the campfire's dying out, then these lambs you mock will burn themselves, just to walk one more mile before the final breath.
No glory sought. No remembrance needed. We'll lie down here, leaving not a trace.
But what's the point of it all?!
The point? You who watch us burn... You're the proof. You, and everyone behind you, that's our meaning.
And one more thing. Whether I deserve to be the President... That's not for your cheap words or your lousy films to decide.
If you're so desperate to tear me down, that only proves I'm doing something right.
Tell me, who's the real desperate one here?
Hehe, you're not the only one with backup. Checkmate.
Hah... Did the others' memories bleed into mine again? How long was I out? The meeting's already over...
What's happening to me... I just want to forget. To erase this wretched Forte.
Erase... right. If I erase the memory of my Forte itself, maybe then, everything will go back to normal.
I'm done with all of it.
She abandoned her title, her right to explore, and now even her own Forte.
She chose to kill the person she used to be, like someone who had drowned, losing both her air and shadow.

Actor Lucilla reenacts this remade life where Lucilla gave up the President's position, her right to explore, and ultimately her Forte. If everything is destined to fade anyway, why not just enjoy fleeting pleasure before disaster strikes? She attempts to negate everything the real Lucilla has accomplished, tempting her to stay in the Dark Side forever. 
But she miscalculated. Lucilla doesn't budge. She tells her exactly what drives her and Startorch Academy to keep moving forward. And with that, the rabbit's illusion shatters.

Dear {PlayerName}, student of Rabelle College of Startorch Academy, greetings! Thank you for your trust. A file transfer is waiting for your approval. To accept, please reply with "Accept Subscription."
Accept Subscription.
Reject Subscription.
[Attachment Received — Startorch Academy Dark Side Investigation Report No. AN25360.]
Reply accepted. Forced file transfer in progress...
[Attachment Received - Startorch Academy Dark Side Investigation Report No. AN25360.]
Got the file? It contains the basic information about our target, Professor Sevi.
He's heading to the camp today for a geological survey. I'll leave the talking to you.
You receive a WavesLine message from Lucilla.
Accept Subscription.
Reject Subscription.
You're here.
Go after it. I'll handle the rest.
Come on. We've still got work to do.
Stay sharp, {PlayerName}. It can take the form of anything in the Dark Side.
There's only one passage here. It must still be around. Stay close.
Told you... It's checkmate this time.
According to our plan...
Should we...
Hmm... We still haven't figured out what the Fractsidus wants with the Dark Side.
For now, this little one's our only lead. Let me try reading its memory first.
Oh? Looks like I don't even need to use my Forte.
I suppose this counts as our first proper meeting, Madam Lucilla?
Proper meeting? You're talking to me through a rabbit from your little hideout. That's what you call "proper"?
Still... thanks for the film. It was tasteless and trashy, but I'll admit, it added a little fun to my job.
You knew I'd catch this rabbit from the start.
And didn't you already know it was a trap meant for you? We both have what the other wants.
What are you trying to do in Lahai-Roi?
Uh-uh. As an educator, you of all people should know better. Finding the answer is the asker's job, you know.
Still... congratulations. You've won this round.
Won? I never agreed to play your little game. And neither did my students. Right, {PlayerName}?
That's right!
You're just flattering yourself!
Flattering myself? That hurts. I worked very hard setting up those trials for you.
If you didn't enjoy them, don't worry. There'll be a next round. And I promise, it'll be far more thrilling.
Because next time, we'll be betting on the very fate of Lahai-Roi. Will it go boom and vanish... or will the flames of Startorch keep burning?
I can't wait. Truly... I can't wait for our next meeting.
Next meeting...
We almost had it...
It's alright. At least we dealt with the source of the Dark Side's anomaly.
Who's the miko just now?
I know you've got many questions, but let's get out of here first.

You and Lucilla seize the rabbit. The Grand Architect speaks through it to deliver a new announcement: "In the next game, we'll be betting on the fate of Lahai-Roi. Will it go boom and vanish, or will the flames of Startorch keep burning?" Then the rabbit disappears. You ask about the Miko you saw earlier, but Lucilla urges you to leave. The Dark Side is unstable, and explanations can wait until you're out.
You said the things in the Dark Side wouldn't hurt them.
Didn't I do what you asked? You wanted to help Sigrika, so I tweaked the Dark Side to help her see herself clearly.
Besides… no one got hurt, right? Your friend, your teacher… and {PlayerName}.
I should thank you, actually. You helped me finish an experiment…
Experiment?
The opposite of the void… How far can memory-type Resonators go before "its" power consumes them?
And what other tricks is this petty Academy hiding?
A miko wielding a sword, hehe…
Come, my sweet darling. We've still got much to do.
What about the rabbit? Isn't it your pet?
We don't keep trash. We dump it.
So… please try hard to be useful, if you don't want to be disposable.

Denia confronts the Grand Architect, reminding them of their promise that the Dark Side would not harm Sigrika or Rover. Unconcerned, the Architect points out that no one was actually hurt in the end. They mockingly thank everyone for their roles in this experiment exploring the opposite of the Void—testing the extent memory-type Resonators can resist "its" power. With that, the Grand Architect leaves with Denia to see to other matters awaiting their attention.
Sorry, {PlayerName}. I've kept you busy all day.
You do owe me an apology.
But not for that.
From the moment you came to me, you already knew this was a trap set by the Fractsidus. Yet, you planned to walk right into it.
All in a day's work for your President. Besides... it worked out pretty well, don't you think?
But what if something had gone wrong?
Don't worry. I know the Dark Side pulls people toward what they fear and try to escape from. But I learned to face my past honestly long ago.
Then… what exactly happened to you back there?
You want to know?
Didn't you say you wanted to introduce a new course, Startorch Guidelines: From Strangers to Confidants?
Students and teachers should get to know each other, right? Maybe this counts as our first class.
Sigh... I really set myself up for that one. Well...
Before I turned twenty, I couldn't tell which memories were mine and which belonged to the ones I had read.
As I absorbed more and more of them, I saw too many births and deaths… I started to feel how small and ignorant I really was.
That's exactly what the Dark Side preyed on. It then recreated the predicament I once lived through. The Fractsidus was questioning me, the representative of Startorch Academy…
When the future offers no answers, and existence itself is destined to fade… can anything we do, in our brief and ignorant lives, truly matter?
As a student of Startorch Academy, what do you think, {PlayerName}?
If there's no answer, then we create one.
Haha, that's the kind of answer you'd say.
Back then, I joined the Spacetrek Collective without a real purpose, but luckily, just like you, I met a good teacher.
My teacher was an ordinary man, a Roya in charge of observation in the frostlands. We both spent our days watching and recording, each in our own realm…
But he never saw himself as just an observer. Again and again, he made connections with people and the things he studied and built bridges between them.
It was like how we draw lines between the stars now. In these perilous frostlands, each person is a point of light for another.
Maybe I used to stand too high, never stooping to feel the emotions in those memories… the ones that needed time to settle for an ordinary person.
When I revisited the memories I once read, I realized they all shared one thing in common—everyone spends their whole life searching for answers.
And it's those very answers that give meaning to our journey toward the future, even if we might find nothing in the end.
Then, have you found your answers?
No… I'm still searching. In that sense, I'm the same as all of you, just another student on the path.
So don't worry. Until I find it, the Fractsidus won't be able to shake me. Also, about that miko lady you're curious about…
My lips are sealed on that, I'm afraid. A President has her principles, not to mention the Academy's safety protocol. But one day, you'll see her again.
Anyway, I still have to write a full report on the Dark Side. Leave the rest to the adults now. It's getting late, {PlayerName}. I'll see you in class.
…Has this game truly ended?

Upon leaving the Dark Side, you reproach Lucilla for acting alone. She calmly explains that her confidence stemmed from having long since learned to confront her past honestly. She shares a story from her youth when, feeling lost, she met a Roya teacher. He taught her how to hold her own memories, respect others', and find meaning in whatever comes next. Noticing your curiosity about the Miko, Lucilla smiles and promises that you will meet her when the time is right.
Hmm? Still something you want to say?
I'd like to hear more about your past.
Oh? But Startorch Guidelines: From Strangers to Confidants is designed for both teachers and students, isn't it?
If you want to hear mine, you'll have to share your stories before coming to Lahai-Roi first.
(Tell her part of your story in Jinzhou)
(Tell her part of your story in Rinascita)
You tell Lucilla part of your story in Jinzhou. She nods occasionally, respectful of the people there.
People everywhere are protecting their homes in their own ways. The Fractsidus's claim that it's all meaningless is the greatest insult to our era.
And your pen pal sounds like a sincere, kind girl. It's a blessing, in times like these, to have someone you can truly talk to.
I hope I get to meet her again.
You will. Human connections are stronger than you think.
You tell Lucilla part of your story in Rinascita. Curiosity flickers in her eyes.
I've only read about Rinascita in textbooks. Their culture of living alongside Echoes reminds me a bit of the Roya.
If I ever get the chance, I'd love to visit there and hear their bards perform in person.
All right, since you shared, I'll tell you something you wanted to know. Let's start with my Royan teacher.
During an expedition in the frostlands, I got caught in a storm and was separated from the others. Due to my chaotic memories, I barely understood the concept of death.
An old Royan observer rescued me and brought me back to his outpost.
When he learned of my ability, he asked me to help him recall the memories he'd long forgotten.
In that small cabin, I read the joys and sorrows of his past.
He studied the behavior of Exoswarm alone, danced the Royan dances there, and shared laughter with passing researchers.
He was never lonely. I began to follow his example and actively reached out to the memories I once read. I wanted to show my respect and experience them anew.
Guess what I felt then?
Awakening?
No. It was the fear of death… For the first time, I truly feared death. I felt their most genuine human emotions facing the jaws of death.
When I realized that, I knew I was finally waking up from the nightmare of those memories.
It's just the two of us now. No need for all the formality, Hiyuki.
Want to try one? It's pretty popular among the students.
Not hungry.
Candy's supposed to be eaten when you're not hungry. That's what makes it taste good. Go on, try it.
Have you swallowed it?
Umm-hmm. A bit sour… and my tongue's tingling. Not my favorite.
Huh? No reaction at all? That's odd. When Sigrika and the others ate theirs, they…
What?
You're no fun.
{Male=He;Female=She} saw me. Does that affect your plan?
It's fine. The Fractsidus didn't just set the trap for me. It was meant to draw you out as well.
They've noticed your existence.
I'm sorry for keeping you confined… for trying to hide your power. In the end, it still came to light.
We both knew this day would come sooner or later.
So… would you consider moving back to the Academy for now? If anything happens, you can respond right away.
The Fractsidus went to great lengths to tamper with the Dark Side, but it poses little actual threat to the Academy.
The wish-granting miko, you—
At worst, this anomaly might be tied to the power of the Threnodian, too. It seems they're already preparing their next phase.
What do you need me to do?
Now's not the time.
You should know that the Spacetrek Collective was built atop the Threnodian. That's why we've prepared countless contingency plans.
Even if every single one failed… even if the Threnodian completely consumed the Collective, we still have a final card to play.
Oh, and just so you know, the maintenance fee for that blade will be coming out of your paycheck this month.
Which blade?
The one you used in the Dark Side. That makes it the fourth you've broken so far. Try to be gentler the next time, will you?
Sure. What about my own sword?
Its maintenance is mostly done. Don't worry, you'll have it back soon.

Sometime later, the mysterious Miko reappears at Lucilla's side. Lucilla calls her  Hiyuki. As they review the incident, Lucilla acknowledges that the Fractsidus likely have bigger schemes in motion. While the Academy has prepared numerous contingency plans, should they fail, the Spacetrek Collective retains one final protocol—Hiyuki.
It's worth noting that while many have entered the Dark Side, only a few were chosen by the rabbit.
While the Celebration incident was a Fractsidus scheme targeting you and Sigrika, the other victims might have followed a pattern that drew the rabbit.
And that pattern lies hidden among the victims themselves.
Exactly. We need to find out what each of them did in the Dark Side and make comparisons.
The common threads might be what attracted the rabbit. The Academy has already investigated the victims.
Based on what we've found, a few possible factors stand out.
Like how long they stayed in the Dark Side, whether they entered with a strong purpose or desire, and whether they brought anything back.
Our next lead is a professor with a pretty strong opinion against the Academy's management. That's why... you, as a student, are a better choice to approach him.
You two don't get along?
What about you?
Not exactly. Some of his beliefs just don't align with the executives' ideas.
Setting that aside, I'll support your investigation from the Dark Side. My Forte will be essential to uncover what they experienced.
Don't worry. I'll be assisting from the Dark Side. My Forte will be essential to uncover what they experienced.
Once you speak with him, try to obtain one of his personal items. That'll let me relive his memories in the Dark Side.
So I'm on the bright side, and you're on the dark...
Take care of yourself in there.
Hm. Now, I've <ano=WavesLine>texted</ano> you the basic info on that victim... And take this.
A makeup mirror?
President Lucilla...?
Don't underestimate this little mirror. It's actually a communicator. It's only natural for partners to have a private line, right?
It's getting late. I'm already on my way to the Dark Side. The on-campus investigation is in your hands now.
Oh, and check your phone. I've sent you a new message.

Lucilla notes that the rabbit's attacks are highly selective. To determine how it chooses its targets, she proposes a coordinated investigation into the victims. You approach the victims and collect key personal belongings, and she will use her Forte to read the memories tied to them. 
Together, you'll dig through what she finds and look for answers.
Professor, about my paper—
You've got a lot of nerve bringing that up.
This mirror...
How am I supposed to use it?
You're holding it too close. Using a communicator like that would make you look too suspicious.
Relax. I already adjusted the settings before giving it to you.
Just keep it in your pocket. The key is to stay calm and look natural.
President, can you still hear me?
Yes... The comms are stable. Let's begin.
This is strange... I was setting up the room according to the riddle the Academy gave me, but now it's...
Who changed the riddle? Who altered the scene? Could it be that rabbit the students keep mentioning?
Either way, I should focus on restoring the room through memory projection. If a student gets misled and causes another accident, it'll be a real headache.
Mmm. So this is Professor Sevi's memory when he first entered the Dark Side. He stayed here quite a while and used his Forte.
From what we just saw, he was one of the staff involved in the New Solar Celebration. He came here to restore the scene that had been tampered with.
But...
Something wrong?
Not exactly. Maybe because I'm a memory-type Resonator too. Something caught my attention.
People think memory-type Resonators like us are masters of memories and emotions, unshaken by illusions or insults.
But no Forte is perfect. When we pour all focus into manipulating memories, information floods the mind.
That's when we're most vulnerable. The anomaly in the Dark Side preys on the mind. If I were a Fractsidus member, I'd strike then...
This projection won't hold for long. Let's move forward.
That... that can't be...
But it can. You've spent all your chips. What else do you have left to trade?
Ah, right. Almost forgot about that. Your greatest honor, wasn't it? Let's see if it's worth anything.
Look at that. You've given everything, and yet, you still can't trade for a single answer.
Or maybe... the scale is the answer. The so-called scientific pursuit at Lahai-Roi contributes nothing to the advance of human civilization.
And of course, it can't change what sleeps beneath Lahai-Roi. You know exactly what I mean.
Enough! You don't understand anything!
Oh? Don't I? You've got nothing left. Oh... but there's still one last thing you can trade.
Your life. Go on! This is the part you idealists always love, isn't it?
...I told you. You don't understand! You're just preying on desperate seekers of truth!
I won't let anyone else enter this room!
Offer something of equal value, and the answer's yours. Look, I've even prepared the stakes for you, everything tied to your research.
Thesis, data... It's all here. So, which one's worth the truth?
See? No weight at all. Your report's worthless.
Try again?
Ah, no, no, no. Far from enough. Is that the extent of your pride in knowledge? Why not wager everything?
I never said the answer was free. Let's make a fair trade.

Professor Sevi found himself trapped in a sealed space, face to face with his own dark self. This shadow version knew Sevi's research had stalled, so it offered a deal: surrender something of equal value to the truth he sought, and the answer would be his. Sevi placed the accomplishments he once took pride in on the scale, but their weight barely registered. Then his dark self tempted him to trade his very life for the truth. 
At that moment, Sevi regained clarity and turned it down. And to keep young students and obsessive researchers from gambling their lives on an unknown answer, he chose to bury what happened and never speak of it.
Since we've found the answer, I'll wait for you here...
Hello? Lucilla?
Look, there's actually one more thing I didn't mention. After the Celebration, every Dark Side victim shared one trait.
They were all memory-type Resonators. And there aren't many of us in the Academy...
Only one remains. You understand what that means now, don't you?
Since the Fractsidus went to such lengths to invite me, well, as the President, how could I turn down the feast?
That student... You seem to trust {Male=him;Female=her} quite a lot.
Mm-hmm. You'll understand once you meet {Male=him;Female=her} yourself. Anyway, how's your progress?
Just as planned. No one else remains in the Dark Side.
But the rabbit's clever. Every room has traces of its frequency. I can't pin down its exact trail.
Heh. If it were easy to catch, their little "feast" wouldn't be half as entertaining, would it?
And remember, if anything unexpected happens, you know what to do.
Focus my mind. Loosen my thoughts. Just a little. Give the threat an opening.
A small price to draw it out. In that false professor's words, a fair trade it is.

There's one more thing Lucilla didn't tell you. Every victim since the celebration ended was a memory-type Resonator. And now, she's the only one left in the Academy who hasn't been targeted. 
Knowing the Fractsidus are coming for her, Lucilla decides to do what Sevi did—use her Forte in the Dark Side, draw the anomaly out, and put an end to this. The moment her signal cuts out, a mysterious Miko appears right beside her.
That's amazing, Professor Lucilla! Oh, should I say President Lucilla now?
Thank you for your trust, but... I don't intend to accept.
Compared to others at the Academy, don't you think I'm... a bit too ordinary?
I have no major academic achievements nor administrative experience. Honestly, sometimes I even feel small standing among all of you.
Making a nobody the shepherd is dangerous for everyone, isn't it?
So... please understand my choice. I'm content following at the back of the flock. I request a new election.
In the remake, Lucilla refuses the position of President, convinced that she's not talented and experienced enough for the role.
Quiet, quiet! Everyone, please be seated.
Hmph. At least she knows her place. Let's just set a new election date and move on.
Look! Professor Lucilla's here!
So, what does your receiver look like?
Why not take a guess?
A lip balm?
Another makeup mirror?
We have a winner. Hehe, nice association.
...Unfortunately, you guessed wrong. Think of something that pairs with a mirror, like a lip balm.
Hah. First time I've ever seen you speechless, Professor.
About your request, I have the right to reject it.
She said nothing. Deep down, she'd already accepted her own mediocrity... and stopped moving forward.
The girl who once forced herself to stand tall now teeters on the edge. Her curtain's about to fall.
How long has she been locking herself in there?
No clue. We just heard stuff breaking... and someone crying.
No sign of it here either. {PlayerName}, let's split up.
I'll check this room. You take the one before.
So, when the launch program for Helios bugged out, how did you manage to recalibrate the parameters? And what about the live Drive data...
Please, one question at a time, Professor.
Ahem... Watching a project come to life always gets me a little overexcited. I helped with part of the launch mechanism myself.
Oh, I haven't even introduced myself properly. I'm Sevi, Professor from the Department of Engineering, and a past recipient of the Spacetrek Collective's Startorch Prize.
This prize hasn't been awarded in years due to the difficult situation. Oh, don't get me wrong. I'm not showing off.
I keep it... to remind myself that our science did make headway before, though now...
...Anyway, {PlayerName}, what brings you here?
(Lucilla mentioned that he's not on good terms with the management. I can't let him know I was sent here by her.)
(Still, we're both victims of the Dark Side. Maybe approaching from that angle won't raise suspicion.)
